rationale: Biology level-3 is organized by the scientific-question-asked, not by method. I tried to capture the working group identities that publish in journals like Cell, Nature, eLife. RNA biology, single-cell methods, organoid biology, CRISPR/genome editing all earn dedicated leaves because labs self-identify with them. I held back on duplicating across L2 parents — e.g. ribosome biology is here under Molecular Biology, not separately under Biochemistry or Structural Biology.

subfields.name: Transcription & Gene Regulation
subfields.tagline: Transcription factors, enhancers, chromatin loops, regulatory grammar.

subfields.name: RNA Biology & Modifications
subfields.tagline: RNA processing, splicing, mRNA modifications, ncRNA.

subfields.name: Chromatin & Epigenetics
subfields.tagline: Histone modifications, DNA methylation, nuclear organization.

subfields.name: DNA Replication, Repair & Genome Stability
subfields.tagline: Replisome, DSB repair, replication stress, R-loops.

subfields.name: Protein Homeostasis & Translation
subfields.tagline: Ribosome, chaperones, ubiquitin-proteasome, autophagy at protein level.

subfields.name: Cell Cycle & Division
subfields.tagline: Mitosis, cytokinesis, cell-cycle checkpoints, spindle assembly.

subfields.name: Organelle Biology & Membrane Trafficking
subfields.tagline: Mitochondria, ER, lysosome, vesicle transport, contact sites.

subfields.name: Cell Death & Stress Responses
subfields.tagline: Apoptosis, pyroptosis, ferroptosis, ER stress.

subfields.name: Cell Signaling & Mechanotransduction
subfields.tagline: Receptor signaling, MAPK/AKT, mechanobiology, ion-channel signaling.

subfields.name: Human Genetics & GWAS
subfields.tagline: GWAS, polygenic scores, rare variants, monogenic disease research.

subfields.name: Functional Genomics & CRISPR Screens
subfields.tagline: Genome-wide CRISPR screens, Perturb-seq, MPRAs.

subfields.name: Single-Cell Genomics & Atlases
subfields.tagline: scRNA-seq, scATAC-seq, spatial transcriptomics, cell atlases.

subfields.name: Population & Comparative Genomics
subfields.tagline: Population genetics inference, ancient DNA, comparative species genomics.

subfields.name: Stem Cell Biology
subfields.tagline: ES/iPS cells, adult stem cells, somatic reprogramming.

subfields.name: Organoids & 3D Models
subfields.tagline: Brain, gut, kidney, vascular organoids — generation and analysis.

subfields.name: Morphogenesis & Pattern Formation
subfields.tagline: Embryonic patterning, gastrulation, organogenesis mechanisms.

subfields.name: Bacterial Physiology & Pathogenesis
subfields.tagline: Bacterial cell biology, virulence, host-pathogen at bacterial side.

subfields.name: Virology (Basic)
subfields.tagline: Viral replication, structural virology, host-virus mechanism.

subfields.name: Host Microbiome
subfields.tagline: Gut/skin/oral microbiome, microbiome-immune, host-microbe gnotobiotic.

subfields.name: Phage Biology & Microbial Defense
subfields.tagline: Phages, CRISPR-Cas defense biology, anti-phage systems.

subfields.name: T-Cell Biology
subfields.tagline: T-cell development, effector differentiation, exhaustion.

subfields.name: Innate Immunity & Inflammation
subfields.tagline: Innate sensing, inflammasomes, macrophages, complement.

subfields.name: Tumor Immunology (Basic)
subfields.tagline: Tumor immune mechanisms, basic immune escape, in vivo modeling.

subfields.name: Mucosal & Tissue Immunity
subfields.tagline: Gut, skin, lung tissue immunity, tissue-resident lymphocytes.

subfields.name: Oncogenic Signaling & Tumor Suppressors
subfields.tagline: KRAS, p53, PI3K/MTOR, Wnt — basic mechanism.

subfields.name: Tumor Microenvironment
subfields.tagline: CAFs, TAMs, ECM, hypoxia, metabolic crosstalk in tumors.

subfields.name: Metastasis Biology
subfields.tagline: Invasion, dissemination, dormancy, organotropism.

subfields.name: Cancer Metabolism
subfields.tagline: Metabolic reprogramming, nutrient dependencies, metabolite signaling.

subfields.name: Enzymology & Catalytic Mechanism
subfields.tagline: Enzyme structure-function, kinetics, allostery.

subfields.name: Metabolism & Metabolomics
subfields.tagline: Metabolic pathway biology, metabolomics, lipidomics.

subfields.name: Chemical Biology
subfields.tagline: Activity-based probes, PROTACs, covalent fragments.

subfields.name: Cryo-EM & Structural Determination
subfields.tagline: Single-particle cryo-EM, cryo-ET, in-situ structure.

subfields.name: Computational Protein Structure & Design
subfields.tagline: AlphaFold-class prediction, de novo design, structure prediction.

subfields.name: ML Methods for Biology
subfields.tagline: Foundation models for biology, sequence models, protein LLMs.

subfields.name: Single-Cell & Spatial Methods (Computational)
subfields.tagline: Tools for scRNA-seq, integration, batch correction, spatial omics analysis.

subfields.name: Synthetic Gene Circuits & Cell Programming
subfields.tagline: Engineered transcription, feedback circuits, cell-state programming.

uncovered_concerns: Held back on splitting Plant Biology and Physiology into their own L3 leaves at full depth — papers there will fold into the closest mechanism leaf (e.g. plant transcription → Transcription & Gene Regulation; cardiac physiology research → Cell Signaling & Mechanotransduction). If volume justifies it later, add a dedicated 'plant-specific signaling' leaf in v2.
Marine & Aquatic Biology L2 has no L3 leaves — its papers will mostly fold under microbiology or population-level leaves until volume warrants its own depth.
Neurobiology L2 has no L3 leaves at this depth — synaptic biology / glia papers fold under Cell Signaling, organelle, etc.
